answer:
**For Charles Dunoyer, "industry" (*industrie*) denotes the skillful, purposeful human activity—rooted in the Latin *industria* (from *intus-struere*, "to construct within")—directed toward useful ends, especially the creation of utilities (values) through labor on things or persons, without violence, spoliation, or exploitation.[^1][^2] It encompasses not merely mechanical or manufacturing arts (as in narrower contemporary usage) but *all* productive professions: agriculture, manufacturing, commerce, sciences, teaching, law, medicine, arts, governance, and moral instruction, provided they generate lasting utilities (e.g., health, knowledge, virtues, order) via peaceful exchange rather than domination.[^3][^4] Dishonest "industries" like fraud ("knights of industry") or privilege-seeking are excluded as non-productive spoliation.[^5] The "industrial regime" (*régime industriel*) is thus the ideal society where universal activity turns toward such labor: no slaves, privileged, or office-seekers; only producers exchanging freely, yielding maximal liberty through moral and productive progress.[^6][^7]**

**Charles Comte employs *industrie* similarly in his broader oeuvre (though less centrally in *Traité de la propriété*), aligning with their shared editorial tradition at *Le Censeur Européen*, where "industriels" designated the peaceful, value-creating class opposing parasitic "décadents" (exploiters via force or privilege).[^8] In *Traité de législation*, he links industry to liberty-enabling property appropriation, contrasting slave-based economies (unproductive) with free labor creating value.[^9]**

**This 19th-century French liberal sense differs sharply from later (post-1830s) connotations, where "industrial" narrowed to mechanized manufacturing, factories, and the "industrial revolution" (e.g., heavy production, urbanization, class conflict per Marx/Saint-Simon). For Comte/Dunoyer, *industrie* is philosophical and universal—any non-violent utility production fostering civilization/liberty—not sector-specific, materialist, or conflict-ridden; it includes intellectual/moral "industries" (e.g., politics as highest) and rejects exploitation outright, prefiguring but transcending physiocratic/agricultural biases.[^10][^11] The corpus lacks coverage of later Marxist/productivist shifts.**

[^1]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie*, VOL I, [ch. 11](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/11-i-liberte-compatible-avec-la-maniere-de-vivre-des-/#p-06038f).

[^2]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie*, VOL I, [ch. 11](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/11-i-liberte-compatible-avec-la-maniere-de-vivre-des-/#p-cd2259).

[^3]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie*, VOL II, [ch. 13](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/#p-76ee09).

[^4]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie*, VOL II, [ch. 13](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/#p-a1cb96).

[^5]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie*, VOL I, [ch. 11](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/11-i-liberte-compatible-avec-la-maniere-de-vivre-des-/#p-b3a2fd).

[^6]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie*, VOL I, [ch. 11](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/11-i-liberte-compatible-avec-la-maniere-de-vivre-des-/#p-872da7).

[^7]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie*, VOL I, [Introduction](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/00-introduction/#p-67acca).

[^8]: *Traité de législation*, VOL I, [ch. 0](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/00-preface/translation/) (contextual; direct "industry" sparse in translations, but doctrinal overlap evident).

[^9]: *Traité de la propriété*, VOL I, [ch. 2](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-i-2c7a99/02-de-la-liberte-consideree-comme-une-condition-de-le/translation/) (liberty enables industrious appropriation).

[^10]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie*, VOL I, [ch. 11](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/11-i-liberte-compatible-avec-la-maniere-de-vivre-des-/#p-5516e9).

[^11]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie*, VOL II, [ch. 13](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/#p-29f8d8).
